text: factors and should be fed to infants. /c63Breast-feeding reduces risk of infections. /c63It establishes mother-infant contact and promotes mother-child bonding. /c63It prolongs birth interval by fertility control (delayed return of menstruation). /c63Breast-feeding helps in retraction of the uterus. /c63Incidence of breast cancer is lower in mothers who breast feed their children. /c63Breast feeding is associated with better cognitive development of children and may provide some long-term health benefits. Breast-milk contains all essential nutrients needed for the infant; it provides the best nutrition and protects the infant from infections. Breast-milk is a natural food and is more easily digested and absorbed by the infant as compared to formula milk prepared from other sources.
